suppressions.reason: Next.js requires unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval for client-side hydration. Fix tracked in story-35.11b (CSP nonce generation).
Next.js requires unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval for client-side hydration. Same root cause as authenticated web scan. Fix tracked in story-35.11b.

FastAPI get_swagger_ui_html() generates inline <script> for SwaggerUIBundle init, requiring unsafe-inline. All other CSP directives are strict (default-src 'none', frame-ancestors 'none'). Assets are self-hosted, no CDN dependency. Cannot eliminate unsafe-inline without replacing FastAPI's docs template.

csrf_token cookie is intentionally non-HttpOnly (httponly=False). Double-submit CSRF pattern requires JavaScript to read the cookie and echo it in the X-CSRF-Token header. Making it HttpOnly would break CSRF protection entirely. This is the OWASP-recommended double-submit cookie pattern.
session_id is a non-sensitive client-generated UUID for disclaimer acknowledgment tracking, not an auth session token. Cannot impersonate users or access protected resources.

CSRF token cookie is intentionally set on all responses (including public endpoints) as part of the double-submit cookie CSRF protection pattern. Clients need the token before their first POST request. ZAP remediation confirms: 'informational alert, nothing to fix.'

No User-Agent-based conditional logic exists in the platform. Response differences detected by ZAP are from path-based routing (mobile vs web endpoints), timing variations, and correlation IDs. No UA-based attack surface.

Content-Type is not required on 302 redirect responses with empty bodies (RFC 7231 Section 3.1.1.1). HTML pages get Content-Type automatically from Next.js. ZAP flags the redirect responses from the auth middleware.

Content-Type is not required on 302 redirect responses with empty bodies (RFC 7231 Section 3.1.1.1). ZAP flags redirect responses from Next.js middleware when unauthenticated users hit protected pages.
Scanner classification note, not a security finding. ZAP detected modern SPA patterns. Remediation text confirms: 'informational alert, no changes required.'

ZAP plugin 10111 ('Authentication Request Identified') is informational -- it lets ZAP's auto-detect set the authentication method to match the identified request. CWE is -1 (N/A). The plugin correctly identifies the project's auth endpoints; no actual vulnerability. Originally flagged on a 2026-04 scan (issues #512/#513, both info-level); those issues auto-close on the next full-suite scan after this suppression lands. Remove this entry if ZAP ever changes plugin 10111 from informational to a real severity.
Same as the zap-api entry above -- ZAP plugin 10111 is informational. The unauthenticated scan also identifies auth endpoints; same rationale applies.
